title: Physics Simulation API - Documentation & Revenue Plan
content:
# Physics Simulation API - Documentation & Revenue Plan

Python
Overview
Working AWS Lambda physics calculation API
Status: Live and functional
Last tested: January 8, 2026, 6 PM PST
Execution time: 11 seconds
Infrastructure: Production-ready (SQS, API Gateway, S3, EventBridge)
What It Does (VERIFIED WORKING)
Calculates real quantum mechanics and classical physics:
Quantum Mechanics
Hydrogen Atom Energy Levels
Photon Energy from Wavelength
Particle in a Box
Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
API Format
Input (JSON):
{
  "simulation_type": "quantum",
  "parameters": {
    "principal_quantum_number": 2,
    "wavelength": 500e-9,
    "box_length": 1e-9,
    "quantum_number": 1,
    "position_uncertainty": 1e-10
  }
}

Output (JSON):
{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "body": {
    "photon_energy": 3.97e-19,
    "frequency": 5.996e14,
    "hydrogen_energy_eV": -3.4,
    "energy_level": 6.02e-20,
    "momentum_uncertainty_min": 5.27e-25,
    "constants_used": {
      "planck_constant": 6.626e-34,
      "speed_of_light": 299792458
    }
  }
}

Revenue Plan (Realistic Path to $120/year minimum)
Week 1 (Jan 9-15, 2026)
Create simple documentation website on S3 ($0.50/mo)
Add 2 more calculation types (orbital mechanics, thermodynamics)
Set up API Gateway with authentication
Week 2 (Jan 16-22)
Integrate Stripe billing
Pricing tiers:
Week 3 (Jan 23-29)
Post on r/AskPhysics: "Free quantum calculator API - need beta testers"
Email 10 physics TAs at UW/WSU
Get 10-20 free users testing
Week 4 (Jan 30 - Feb 5)
Launch paid tiers
Target: 1 paying customer = SUCCESS
$120/year minimum = AWS costs covered
Technical Details
AWS Resources:
Lambda function: physics-simulation-engine
Region: us-west-2
Memory: 128 MB
Timeout: 15 minutes
Function ARN: arn:aws:lambda:us-west-2:861870144562:function:physics-simulation-engine
Connected Services:
SQS: Batch job processing
API Gateway: 2 REST endpoints
EventBridge: Scheduled runs
S3: File storage for results
Physics Constants (CODATA 2018):
Planck constant: 6.62607015×10⁻³⁴ J·s
Speed of light: 299,792,458 m/s
Gravitational constant: 6.67430×10⁻¹¹ m³/kg·s²
Boltzmann constant: 1.380649×10⁻²³ J/K
Avogadro constant: 6.02214076×10²³ mol⁻¹
Next Steps (Priority Order)
TODAY: Finish this documentation
Tomorrow: Create S3 website with API examples
This weekend: Add orbital mechanics calculations
Next week: Set up Stripe + launch free beta
End of month: Get first paying customer
Contact for Testing
Reddit: r/AskPhysics, r/Physics
Universities: UW Physics, WSU Physics, Bellevue College
Target: Physics students, TAs, researchers
Last updated: January 8, 2026 by Izack ThorneLocation: Port Angeles, WA

This implements the SpiralRing-64 with exponential expansion:
